LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PEBBLEBROOK HOTEL TRUST AND DONS HOTEL OWNER LP'S MOTION TO DISMISS HOTEL ZEPHYR IS GRANTED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFF RAMONA SALAS FILING A MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND TO ADD HOTEL ZEPHYR AS A DEFENDANT AND/OR JUDGMENT DEBTOR IN THE EVENT SHE LEARNS FACTS THAT HOTEL ZEPHYR IS AN ENTITY THAT CAN BE SUED. DEFENDANTS HAVE ADEQUATELY SHOWN THAT HOTEL ZEPHYR IS NOT A LEGAL ENTITY AND THUS IT CANNOT BE SUED. IF MS. SALAS LEARNS INFORMATION TO THE CONTRARY, SHE MAY SEEK TO AMEND HER COMPLAINT OR ANY JUDGMENT THAT SHE MIGHT OBTAIN TO ADD HOTEL ZEPHYR AND MAY DO SO WITHOUT REGARD TO ANY STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS OR LACHES DEFENSE DUE TO PRINCIPLES OF JUDICIAL ESTOPPEL. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: G. GONZALES; REPORTER: GISELLE CASEY CSR#8090, EMAIL: CASEY.GISELLE@GMAIL.COM
ANSWER (TO X-COMPLAINT OF PEBBLEBROOK HOTEL TRUST FILED ON NOV 29, 17 IN CASE # CGC-560999) (TRANSACTION ID # 61547481) FILED BY DEFENDANT SCHINDLER ELEVATOR CORPORATION

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-28-2018 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-08-2018 Set For Trial On Oct-01-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Hotel Zephyr
Off Calendar Jan-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michelle D. Jew, Esq. of Burnham Brown, T:510.835.6715, for defendant and moving party;
Jordan Meeks, Esq. of Archer Norris, T:925.930.6600, for defendant Schindler, by Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS PEBBLEBROOK HOTEL TRUST and DONS HOTEL OWNER LP'S Motion To Dismiss Hotel Zephyr is GRANTED WITHOUT PREJUDICE to plaintiff Ramona Salas filing a motion for leave to amend to add Hotel Zephyr as a defendant and/or judgment debtor in the event she learns facts that Hotel Zephyr is an entity that can be sued. Defendants have adequately shown that Hotel Zephyr is not a legal entity and thus it cannot be sued. If Ms. Salas learns information to the contrary, she may seek to amend her complaint or any judgment that she might obtain to add Hotel Zephyr and may do so without regard to any statute of limitations or laches defense due to principles of judicial estoppel.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Giselle Casey CSR#8090, email: casey.giselle@gmail.com

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-15-2017 Continued To Feb-21-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-17-560999 WITH CASE CGC-16-554666
Off Calendar Dec-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Jordan M. Meeks of the law offices of Archer Norris (650-761-3727) appears as counsel for defendan/tnon-opposing party in Case CGC-17-560999, Schindler Elevator Corporation

No other appearance is made in this matter

There being no opposition, the Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF RAMONA SALAS' MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CGC-17-560999 and CGC-16-554666 is granted. The two cases are consolidated for all purposes. The lead case is CGC-16-554666 and all further filings must be made in in that case only. The January 3, 2018 case management conference set in case CGC-16-554666 remains on calendar, while the January 31, 2018 case management conference set in case CGC-17-560999 is vacated. Ms. Salas is granted leave to file a new pleading no later than December 15, 2017 bearing both case numbers entitled "Complaint in Consolidated Cases," which supersedes the complaints she filed in the two cases. Doing so will allow the relation back doctrine to apply and potentially avoid statute of limitations issues if one of the two cases were dismissed, while making clear that this is truly one case and must be tried as a single case. Defendants are required to file responses to the new pleading within two weeks of receiving it. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR No. 8098, e-mail: casey.giselle@gmail.com. (L&M302)
